relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Moses shepherds beyond the wilderness and arrives at the mountain of Divine.
Fire burns in the bush, yet the bush is not consumed.
Moses turns aside toward wonder.
When he turns, the Divine calls his name from the flame.
Sandals come off because wilderness ground has become holy.
The ancestral Divine speaks, and Moses hides his face.
YHWH sees affliction, hears the cry, and knows the pain.
The Divine comes down to rescue and bring up into spaciousness.
The cry has arrived; oppression is seen.
Moses is sent to Pharaoh for liberation.
Moses asks, Who am I?
The answer is presence: I will be with you.
Moses asks for the Name.
The Name opens as I AM / I WILL BE.
YHWH is remembered from generation to generation.
The elders are gathered around the visitation of YHWH.
Affliction will be lifted toward milk-and-honey spaciousness.
The elders go with Moses to ask for wilderness worship.
YHWH knows Pharaoh will resist.
Wonders will break open the sending-away.
The people will not leave empty.
The wealth of oppression is drawn out for the children.

contemplative_note: Exodus 3 is the flame of liberation: holy ground, the Name as presence, and the Divine who sees, hears, knows, comes down, and sends.
